cards.title: Notion Restores Anthropic Access After Disruption
cards.body: Notion's head of product expressed surprise at the viral attention surrounding the service disruption. The access has now been restored following the outage.

cards.title: OpenAI Shifts Strategy Beyond Chat Interface
cards.body: A senior OpenAI employee stated that 'Chat is dead' as the company pivots toward building a 'super app.' The company continues development on this ambitious new direction.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Lockdown Mode for Data Protection
cards.body: Lockdown Mode aims to reduce prompt injection vulnerabilities and protect sensitive data in ChatGPT. While not eliminating risk entirely, the feature significantly strengthens security.

cards.title: Apple Intelligence Updates Coming at WWDC 2026
cards.body: Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference will showcase Siri's highly anticipated revamp and new Apple Intelligence features. The announcements are expected to drive major updates to AI capabilities.

cards.title: Sriram Krishnan Departs White House AI Role
cards.body: The White House AI advisor is stepping down to launch a new institution focused on AI policy. Krishnan will continue shaping the Trump administration's approach to AI.

cards.title: Trump Administration Considers OpenAI Equity Stake
cards.body: President Trump is discussing potential deals where Americans could benefit from AI's success. The proposal would give the U.S. government a financial stake in OpenAI.

cards.title: Startup Battlefield 200 Applications Close June 8
cards.body: Applications for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026's Startup Battlefield 200 close on June 8 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Winners will compete on the Disrupt Stage in San Francisco this October.

cards.title: Google Pays SpaceX $920M Monthly for Compute
cards.body: Google has inked a major deal with SpaceX valued at $920 million per month for computational capacity. The agreement reflects unexpected demand for Google's recently launched AI products.

cards.title: Startups Push Back Against Phone-First Culture
cards.body: A wave of startups is intentionally steering users away from smartphones toward in-person experiences. Board, a new social gaming startup, exemplifies this 'together tech' movement gaining traction.

cards.title: AI Industry Shifts Focus to Cost Control
cards.body: The conversation around AI has pivoted from 'tokenmaxxing' to implementing guardrails and cost management. Rising AI expenses are forcing companies to reassess their growth-at-all-costs strategies.

cards.title: 'Together Tech' Wave Defines 2026 Startup Bets
cards.body: Founders are increasingly building products that bring people together through in-person experiences rather than screen time. This countermovement marks a significant shift in startup priorities and fundraising.

cards.title: AirTrunk Commits $30B to India Data Centers
cards.body: The Australian operator will build 5GW of AI data center capacity in India. This massive investment reflects surging demand for computational infrastructure across Asia.

cards.title: Mira Murati Returns to Spotlight Amid Competition
cards.body: The executive is becoming more visible after maintaining a lower profile in recent months. In today's crowded market, increased visibility is crucial for maintaining relevance.

cards.title: Anthropic Revenue Surges to $47B Annualized
cards.body: Anthropic announced record annualized revenue of $47 billion in May, up from $9 billion in late 2025. The company faces mounting pressure to justify these returns ahead of its IPO.

cards.title: Airbnb CEO Launches New AI Lab Initiative
cards.body: Brian Chesky announced plans to build an AI lab as existing LLM partnerships haven't met Airbnb's standards. The company has waited for more mature AI products before committing to partnerships.

cards.title: StrictlyVC LA Summit on Defense and AI
cards.body: A major tech event will gather investors, founders, and leaders on June 18 in Los Angeles. The forum will focus on venture capital, defense tech, and AI's role in industry.

cards.title: Meta Slashes Data Center Costs With Tent Design
cards.body: Meta is building data centers inside tents to dramatically reduce infrastructure expenses. The unconventional approach addresses the company's massive computational demands more cost-effectively.

cards.title: Apple Approves Poke as First Business AI Agent
cards.body: Poke has become the first AI agent approved for Apple's Messages for Business platform. The approval allows users to interact with AI agents through simple text messaging.

cards.title: Meta Rolls Out AI Creator Assistant on Facebook
cards.body: Creators can now use AI to answer questions about posting timing and audience feedback. The assistant simplifies performance analysis by eliminating the need to parse complex dashboards.

cards.title: Hello Robot Releases Fourth-Generation Stretch Unit
cards.body: Hello Robot unveiled the latest version of its Stretch home assistance robot. The California startup is positioning itself to bring robotics into mainstream household use.

cards.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after disruption
cards.body: Notion's head of product noted surprising engagement around the service restoration. The incident highlighted dependencies between major AI platforms.

cards.title: OpenAI working on 'super app' beyond chat
cards.body: A senior OpenAI employee stated "Chat is dead," signaling shift beyond conversational interfaces. The company is exploring expanded AI applications.

cards.title: OpenAI launches Lockdown Mode for data protection
cards.body: The new security feature aims to reduce prompt injection attack vulnerabilities in ChatGPT. It protects sensitive data from being inadvertently shared.

cards.title: Sriram Krishnan leaves White House AI advisor role
cards.body: The former advisor is starting a new institution to continue shaping AI policy. His departure marks a transition in Trump administration AI governance.

cards.title: Trump administration considering OpenAI equity stake
cards.body: President Trump discussed deals allowing Americans to benefit from AI success. The potential investment represents government involvement in major AI companies.

cards.title: Google pays SpaceX $920M monthly for compute
cards.body: The deal stems from unexpected demand for Google's recently launched AI products. It reflects massive infrastructure needs for AI operations.

cards.title: AI industry scrambles to manage runaway costs
cards.body: The conversation shifted from aggressive scaling to implementing guardrails and cost controls. Token costs are forcing industry-wide budget reassessment.

cards.title: AirTrunk commits $30B for AI data centers
cards.body: The Australian operator plans 5GW capacity expansion in India specifically for AI. It demonstrates accelerating infrastructure investment for AI workloads.

cards.title: Anthropic revenue hits $47B annualized rate
cards.body: The AI company achieved dramatic growth from $9B at end of 2025. Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic demonstrates strong market momentum.

cards.title: Airbnb CEO plans to launch new AI lab
cards.body: Brian Chesky stated existing LLM products weren't ready for partnership last year. The company now moves forward with dedicated AI research.

cards.title: Poke becomes first AI agent on Apple Messages
cards.body: The startup gained approval for its text-based AI agent platform on Apple's business channel. It marks growing integration of AI assistants in mainstream messaging.

cards.title: Meta launches AI creator assistant on Facebook
cards.body: The assistant answers creator questions about posting timing and audience comments. It streamlines analytics interpretation through conversational AI.

cards.title: Meta hack exploits AI customer support vulnerability
cards.body: Attackers used Meta's AI agent to steal Instagram accounts by changing associated emails. The breach revealed security gaps in customer-facing AI systems.

cards.title: AI chatbots impact human attention and cognition
cards.body: Psychologist Gloria Mark explores how AI interactions affect user focus and mental control. Studies show concerning effects on cognitive autonomy.

cards.title: Courts face flood of AI-generated lawsuits
cards.body: Judge Maritza Braswell handles increasing volumes of AI-written legal documents from unrepresented litigants. The system struggles to manage quality and legitimacy.

cards.title: Google funds virtual power plants for data centers
cards.body: The company signed a deal with Voltus supporting the largest US power grid's VPP. This addresses massive energy demands from AI operations.

cards.title: Trump signs new executive order on AI
cards.body: The administration issued revised AI policy after scrapping an earlier version. The order reshapes federal AI governance and oversight.

cards.title: Agentic AI could transform global healthcare
cards.body: AI agents address chronic underinvestment and care gaps in healthcare systems. They enable distributed care and bridge service fragmentation.

cards.title: Small businesses leverage AI for operations
cards.body: AI handles accounting, design, research, and product development functions. It democratizes capabilities previously requiring specialized staff.

cards.title: LLMs resist Russian propaganda better than humans
cards.body: Study found certain large language models effectively resist disinformation manipulation. Results suggest AI potential for information warfare defense.
